vitals.participation_trend: consistently full, sells out months in advance

course_description.overview: Non-competitive cyclosportive looping around Lake Geneva (Lac Léman) through Swiss and French vineyards, shores, and hills, with three distances: 176 km full loop (Lausanne-Lausanne), 112 km (Evian-Lausanne), 64 km (Lausanne-Evian). Roads remain open to traffic.

key_segments.name: Lavaux vineyards
key_segments.description: Terraced vineyards and lake views on Swiss side.

key_segments.name: Chablais region
key_segments.description: Hilly terrain near Evian with Mont Blanc vistas.

key_segments.name: Full lake loop climbs
key_segments.description: Cumulative 890 m elevation through rolling hills and lake-hugging roads.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Demanding for full 176 km due to length and 890 m gain over varied terrain; accessible options for shorter routes. No tri bars or TT bikes allowed for safety.

logistics.getting_there: Accessible by car, train, or public transport to Lausanne/Evian; dedicated parking, boat transfers (free for mid-distance participants), shuttles available.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels in Lausanne and Evian; book early due to popularity.
logistics.registration_details: Opens January 15, 2026 at 11h; sells out fast (previous edition full 3+ months early); fees 60-139 CHF; includes jersey, timing, finisher diploma, boat transfer, aid.
logistics.bike_logistics: Bib/maillot pickup at Place de la Navigation, Lausanne; secured bike park at finish; participants responsible for bike maintenance.
logistics.local_considerations: Routes open to traffic, obey road rules; age 16+; date shifted to October due to G7 in Evian; non-competitive, no support vehicles.

final_verdict.one_liner: Switzerland's largest non-competitive cyclosportive: stunning Lake Geneva loop for all levels.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Recreational cyclists seeking epic scenery without racing pressure; endurance riders tackling 176 km.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for notable lake/mountain views, inclusive vibe, and high value inclusions.
pro_tips: Train for 890 m gain on rolling roads.
Use boat transfer for logistics ease.
Respect open roads and no tri bars rule.
